summary: Docker-based PoC demonstrates that the /workflows/import-from-url endpoint can fetch internal network resources by accessing a mock metadata server that simulates cloud IAM credentials.
expected_security_impact: The PoC should demonstrate accessing an internal metadata endpoint (simulating 169.254.169.254) through the workflow import URL parameter, proving SSRF capability.
actual_observed_result: Successfully created an isolated Docker network with n8n and a mock metadata server. The workflow import endpoint was able to fetch data from the internal server at http://172.28.0.100:8080/latest/meta-data/iam/security-credentials/admin-role, demonstrating SSRF access to internal network resources.
verification_method: The run.sh script sets up two containers on an isolated Docker network, attempts to import a workflow from an internal URL pointing to the mock metadata server, and checks if the response contains the simulated IAM credentials.
evidence_produced_by_run: Mock metadata server started on isolated network (172.28.0.100:8080)
n8n container started on same network (172.28.0.10)
Workflow import endpoint accepted URL parameter pointing to internal IP
n8n fetched data from internal metadata server through the vulnerable endpoint
Response contained simulated AWS IAM credentials from the metadata endpoint

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh builds n8n from source using the Dockerfile that clones the repository
